event: John Drowning is Freed by the Virgin Annunciate
symbols: Mary's outstretched hand pulling the man from peril — the intercession of the Blessed Virgin rescuing the faithful
crown of stars around Mary's head — the Woman of Revelation 12, the Queen of Heaven
blue mantle — heavenly grace and the covering of Mary's protection
drowning man reaching upward — human desperation turning to divine aid
dark stormy landscape — the perils of life from which the faithful seek heavenly deliverance
description: A vertical painting in a wooden frame showing a tall standing female figure in a pink dress and deep blue mantle, with a halo or crown of stars around her head. She looks down with a compassionate expression and extends her right hand toward a nude or semi-nude male figure who kneels or crouches at her feet, grasping her hand. The man appears to be in distress, emerging from or rescued from some peril. The background is a dark, atmospheric landscape with stormy skies. An inscription or plaque is visible at the base of the composition.
theological_description: This devotional painting depicts a Marian miracle — the Virgin Mary rescuing a drowning man through her intercession. While not a canonical biblical event, the scene draws on the deep scriptural tradition of divine rescue from the waters. The Psalms repeatedly use drowning as a metaphor for mortal peril and God's salvation: 'Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck... I have come into deep waters, and the flood sweeps over me' (Psalm 69:1-2). God's response: 'He reached down from on high and took hold of me; he drew me out of deep waters' (Psalm 18:16). In Catholic theology, Mary functions as a channel of this divine grace — not as an independent savior but as an intercessor who brings the prayers of the faithful before God. Her role as advocate is rooted in her intercession at Cana, where she brought the needs of others to Jesus: 'They have no wine' (John 2:3), prompting his first miracle. The crown of stars identifies her with the Woman of Revelation 12:1, clothed with the sun with a crown of twelve stars. The scene embodies the theology of Luke 1:48-49: 'All generations will call me blessed; for he who is mighty has done great things for me.'

scripture_refs.reason: The cry of the drowning man for salvation from deep waters is the existential situation depicted
God reaching down and drawing the afflicted from deep waters, enacted through Mary's outstretched hand
Mary's intercession at Cana is the scriptural foundation for her role as intercessor shown here
The crown of stars identifies Mary with the Woman clothed with the sun

_meta.title: John drowning is freed by the Virgin Annunciate
